title: Align PSFN Amica channel and session contract
description:
Resolve the identity and transport contract between Amica, PSFN Satellite Hub, and PSFN Framework before deeper implementation. Current mismatch: Amica filters satellite bridge SSE events by event.data.sessionId === config("psfn_channel_id"), while Satellite Hub AmicaBridge currently sets sessionId from the realtime hub session id. Framework external channel claims require X-PSFN-Channel-Type to be allowlisted as psfn-amica and channel ids to start with psfn-amica:, while Satellite Hub defaults to satellite.endpoint.

Decisions to make:
- Whether Amica should identify the visual companion surface by Framework channel id (psfn-amica:<id>), hub realtime session id (realtime:<device>), or a separate embodied session id.
- Whether Satellite Hub should post Amica bridge events directly to Amica HTTP endpoints or whether Amica should connect as a normal realtime websocket satellite.
- Whether browser/Tauri Amica can submit typed turns and camera/vision uploads directly to Satellite Hub, or only render turns owned by other satellites.
- Which headers/auth tokens are required for SSE GET, bridge POST, mic proxy, and any future websocket path.
- The canonical event vocabulary for user text, assistant audio segments/final, interrupt, face/gaze, expression, animation, viseme, and vision request/result.
acceptance_criteria: A short contract note exists in the bead comments or docs that gives concrete names and examples for channelType, channelId, sessionId, turnId, satelliteId, and token handling. The downstream implementation beads can use that contract without guessing. Any required changes outside psfn-amica are explicitly called out for psfn-satellite-hub or psfn-framework.
close_reason: Aligned bead graph with building-satellites.md realtime satellite contract

comments.author: axAilotl
comments.text:
Aligned against /mnt/samesung/ai/psfn-satellite-hub/docs/building-satellites.md.

Expected shape is realtime satellite, not the current HTTP/SSE bridge as the main path:
- Amica connects to Satellite Hub over WebSocket and sends hello with stable device/session/satellite identity.
- hello.ack is authoritative for resolved session/channel/capabilities.
- Hub owns Hub -> PSFN registry/scalar headers; Amica should not speak the Framework channel-claim contract directly in conduit mode.
- Capabilities should advertise text/subtitle/streamed_audio/animation/expression/gaze by default; vision_upload stays off until a real upload path exists.
- The existing /api/satelliteBridge HTTP/SSE route is compatibility/development plumbing only.

title: Add PSFN Amica integration test harness
description:
Build tests that prove the thin conduit behavior without requiring live PSFN, Satellite Hub, microphones, or GPUs. Existing tests are minimal Jest unit tests. Add focused tests around config, bridge validation, satellite playback reducer/controller, and UI menu selection.

Suggested coverage:
- Config/profile defaults and deployment-owned overrides in conduit vs legacy mode.
- /api/satelliteBridge validation and auth for user.final, assistant.segment/final, interrupt, face.target/clear, malformed audio, unsupported event type.
- Satellite playback behavior: turn registration, segment ordering, duplicate/stale drops, interrupt cleanup, base64 audio decode, text bubble merging.
- Settings/menu key selection in conduit mode.
- Vision routing decision once the contract exists.

Prefer pure Jest/unit tests for controller logic. Add browser/e2e smoke later only if needed to validate canvas/VRM rendering.
acceptance_criteria: npm test covers the PSFN conduit behavior added by this epic without external services. Tests fail if conduit mode starts local AI provider paths or if bridge event validation regresses. The test harness has clear fixtures for representative Satellite Hub events.
close_reason: Added conduit config, bridge, realtime, playback, menu, vision, and home-runtime tests; fixed stale test fixture

title: Disable Amica Life memory and external API loops in conduit mode
description:
Stop Amica Life, reasoning-engine, subconscious, external API, and development dataHandlerStorage behaviors from running as companion brain features in PSFN conduit mode. PSFN Framework owns memory, identity, prompts, and post-turn internal work.

Current hotspots:
- src/pages/index.tsx initializes amicaLife and always calls handleChatLogs(chatLog).
- src/features/amicaLife/* can generate idle text/news/subconscious/function-calling behavior.
- src/features/externalAPI/* persists config/logs/subconscious/user inputs/chat logs in development and exposes /api/amicaHandler and /api/mediaHandler.
- config defaults amica_life_enabled to true.

Preserve animation-only value where useful: idle VRMA/procedural motion can remain, but text/news/subconscious/memory calls should not run unless explicitly enabled for legacy mode.
acceptance_criteria: In PSFN conduit mode, Amica does not write chat logs/user inputs/subconscious through externalAPI, does not generate Amica Life text/news/subconscious events, and does not use reasoning_engine. Optional animation-only idle behavior is preserved or separately controlled. Tests verify initialization paths skip these systems in conduit mode.
close_reason: Gated Amica Life initialization and external chat log writes in conduit mode with tests

title: Route camera and vision through PSFN ownership
description:
Preserve camera/vision capability while moving actual vision reasoning out of Amica local provider configuration for PSFN deployments. Current Amica has EmbeddedWebcam, local getVisionResponse in Chat, OpenAI/Llama/Ollama vision backends, and mediaHandler for external API image processing. PSFN conduit mode should capture images or camera frames and route them to the agreed PSFN Satellite Hub/Framework path.

Work scope:
- Keep camera UI/capture primitives available.
- Decide whether image upload goes to Satellite Hub websocket/HTTP, Framework OpenAI-compatible vision endpoint, or a new bridge event.
- In conduit mode, avoid using config vision_openai/vision_llamacpp/vision_ollama directly unless explicitly configured for fallback/debug.
- Preserve local face tracking separately from semantic vision; face tracking can stay local or receive hub face.target events.
- Ensure captured images are bounded, typed, and do not leak through localStorage/config sync.
acceptance_criteria: A PSFN conduit deployment can capture a camera frame and route it through the PSFN-owned vision path, or the UI clearly marks vision as pending if the hub/framework contract is not ready. Local legacy vision providers remain available outside conduit mode. Tests cover the routing decision and payload bounds where possible.
close_reason: Guarded conduit vision routing, preserved camera capture, and added payload/routing tests

title: Preserve and expose VRM model variant and animation controls
description:
Keep Amica valuable as the visual embodiment surface. PSFN has one companion identity, but VRM files are model variants/outfits and animations are presentation capabilities, not separate companions.

Current code to preserve and clarify:
- src/components/settings/CharacterModelPage.tsx and vrmStore/Dexie local model storage.
- src/components/settings/CharacterAnimationPage.tsx, config animation_url, animation_procedural.
- src/lib/VRMAnimation and viewer/model playAnimation/loadAnimation.
- ProceduralAnimation, EmoteController, LipSync, AutoBlink/AutoLookAt.

Work scope:
- Ensure conduit-mode settings keep model and animation pages.
- Decide whether deployment-owned env can pin allowed model variants or model list source.
- Add satellite event handling for expression/animation/gaze/viseme if the contract includes those events.
- Keep idle/procedural animation available even when Amica Life text/memory behavior is disabled.
- Make model switching safe while satellite audio or face tracking is active.
acceptance_criteria: PSFN conduit mode keeps model/outfit switching and animation selection. Incoming expression/animation events, if selected by contract, drive the VRM without invoking local AI. Idle/procedural animation can run independently of Amica Life LLM/memory features. Tests or a documented manual smoke cover model load, animation load, expression, and audio lip sync after a model switch.
close_reason: Preserved conduit model/animation settings, documented VRM boundaries, and added smoke check

title: Implement Amica outbound satellite client path
description:
If the contract decision says Amica should attach to PSFN Satellite Hub as a normal satellite, implement the browser/Tauri-side outbound client. Satellite Hub already documents a realtime websocket protocol in src/ts/shared/protocol.ts with hello, user.text, interrupt, relay.stt, relay.tts, capabilities, session.ready, hello.ack, message, text, audio, and action events.

Amica-side responsibilities:
- Advertise capabilities appropriate for a VRM presentation surface: text input if enabled, vision_upload if camera capture is enabled, streamed_audio/subtitle/expression/gaze/animation output, interrupt/mute/session_attach controls.
- Send hello with stable deviceId/satelliteId/satelliteName/session/channel identity from config.
- Route typed text, camera/vision submissions, and interrupt/mute actions through the hub instead of directly calling PSFN Framework in conduit mode.
- Reduce incoming websocket message/audio events into the same playback/display path used by the HTTP+SSE bridge, or explicitly decide that HTTP+SSE remains the only first implementation path.

This bead may close as a documented no-op if the resolved contract keeps Amica purely as an HTTP+SSE render target for the first milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Either Amica has a tested outbound hub client for hello/user.text/interrupt/vision-capability basics, or the contract decision explicitly says this is deferred and all UI controls that would require outbound hub ownership are hidden/disabled. No duplicate PSFN calls happen for one user utterance.
close_reason: Added browser WebSocket satellite client, hello/user.text/interrupt handling, and realtime tests

title: Separate satellite playback from local AI chat pipeline
description:
Refactor or fence src/features/chat/chat.ts so PSFN conduit mode does not invoke Amica local LLM/TTS/STT/memory paths for normal turns. The class currently mixes local chat streaming, TTS job generation, browser audio playback, satellite SSE consumption, Amica Life interactions, external API logging, and vision follow-up prompts.

Desired behavior in conduit mode:
- User/assistant display state can be updated from Satellite Hub events.
- Assistant audio supplied by the hub is decoded and passed to viewer.model.speak for lip sync and expression.
- Interrupt clears pending external audio and stops speaking without cancelling nonexistent local readers.
- Local ttsJobs/fetchAudio are not used for satellite-owned assistant turns.
- Manual typed input either routes through the chosen hub/framework contract or is hidden/disabled until that route exists.
- Legacy non-conduit Chat behavior remains intact for debugging.

This can be done by extracting a satellite playback controller or by adding a narrow conduit-mode branch, but the result should make ownership obvious.
acceptance_criteria: In conduit mode, receiving user.final and assistant.segment/final events updates UI, chat log, speech state, lip sync, and interrupt handling without calling getChatResponseStream, fetchAudio, Amica Life memory/subconscious, or external API logging. Unit tests cover satellite turn registration, duplicate/stale segment rejection, final/segment text joining, and interrupt cleanup.
close_reason: Separated conduit Hub playback from local AI stream/TTS paths and added satellite playback tests

title: Trim settings and main controls for PSFN mode
description:
Reshape the Amica UI in PSFN conduit mode so operators see the controls that still matter: model/outfit selection, animation selection/procedural animation, background/render tuning, face tracking/camera/vision controls, mic mute/status from Satellite Hub, debug/status, and language if needed. Hide or collapse legacy AI provider surfaces that PSFN Framework owns.

Current hotspots:
- src/pages/index.tsx shows chat log, TTS mute, webcam, face tracking, share/import, subconscious, debug, chat mode, stream toggles, settings, and Moshi path.
- src/components/settings.tsx main menu exposes Amica Life, ChatBot, TTS, STT, Vision provider pages, External API, Community, and many provider-specific pages.
- src/components/settings/common.tsx has page labels/icons for all legacy pages.

Do not remove model/animation controls: PSFN has one companion, but VRM files are variants/outfits and need to remain switchable.
acceptance_criteria: In PSFN conduit mode, settings and main controls no longer advertise Amica-owned LLM/STT/TTS/memory/provider setup as normal user choices. Appearance, character model, character animation, vision/camera capture, face tracking, mic mute/status, and debug/status remain reachable. Non-conduit mode keeps the existing broader Amica settings. UI tests or focused component tests verify the menu key sets for conduit and non-conduit modes.
close_reason: Trimmed conduit-mode settings menus and added menu key tests

title: Add PSFN conduit runtime profile
description:
Introduce an explicit PSFN conduit mode/profile in Amica configuration. The profile should make production PSFN deployments behave as a presentation client instead of a standalone AI assistant.

Likely shape:
- Add a single mode flag such as NEXT_PUBLIC_AMICA_RUNTIME_MODE=psfn_conduit or NEXT_PUBLIC_PSFN_CONDUIT_MODE=true, plus a helper in src/utils/config.ts so components do not duplicate string checks.
- In conduit mode, default chatbot_backend, tts_backend, stt_backend, amica_life_enabled, reasoning_engine_enabled, external_api_enabled, wake word, local storage sync, and introduction surfaces to non-invasive values.
- Preserve deployment-owned overrides for VRM URL/hash, animation URL/procedural mode, background, language, face tracking, render tuning, PSFN channel/session identifiers, and satellite bridge settings.
- Keep local legacy backends available outside conduit mode for debugging/upstream compatibility.
- Avoid silently rewriting user localStorage in a way that traps non-PSFN dev sessions.
acceptance_criteria: A typed/helper-level config API can answer whether the app is in PSFN conduit mode. Fresh PSFN env defaults do not start local LLM/STT/TTS/memory/external API work. Existing non-PSFN behavior remains available when conduit mode is off. Unit tests cover default config and deployment-owned override behavior.
close_reason: Added PSFN conduit runtime defaults, deployment-owned override behavior, and config tests

title: PSFN Amica thin VRM conduit
description:
Turn this Amica fork into a PSFN-owned companion presentation surface. Amica should keep the 3D/VRM rendering, animation, model/outfit switching, lip sync, camera/vision capture, face/gaze behavior, and compact operator UI. It should stop acting like a standalone AI stack for normal PSFN deployments: PSFN Framework owns LLM, memory, identity, system prompt, STT, TTS, and long-lived companion state; PSFN Satellite Hub owns device transport, mic/speaker hardware, turn endpointing, interrupt behavior, and fan-out to embodied satellites.

Current code review notes:
- src/pages/index.tsx still initializes the full Amica Chat and Amica Life paths and exposes local AI controls by default.
- src/utils/config.ts has PSFN-specific keys, but defaults still favor local/OpenAI/Piper/Whisper behavior.
- src/features/chat/chat.ts already has a psfn chat backend, satellite SSE consumption, browser audio playback/lip sync, and interrupt support.
- src/pages/api/satelliteBridge/* exposes POST-to-SSE relay and mic proxy endpoints.
- src/features/psfnSatelliteBridge/types.ts mirrors part of satellite-hub/src/ts/pi-client/amica-bridge.ts.
- PSFN Framework currently allowlists external channel type psfn-amica, while Amica currently validates channelId prefix from NEXT_PUBLIC_PSFN_CHANNEL_TYPE. Satellite Hub uses satellite.endpoint by default for its own realtime sessions.

The implementation should prefer a deployment/profile mode over deleting useful upstream features immediately, so development can keep fallback/debug surfaces while production PSFN mode is lean.
acceptance_criteria: A PSFN deployment can load Amica as a browser/Tauri presentation surface, connect to Satellite Hub/Framework, receive user/assistant turn events, play assistant audio with lip sync, render expressions/animations/model variants, and use camera/vision routing without invoking Amica local LLM/STT/TTS/memory by default. The bd child graph documents all required code, contract, test, and documentation work with explicit dependencies and no cycles.

title: Document PSFN Amica deployment and ownership boundaries
description:
Replace or supplement upstream Amica-centric setup docs with PSFN-specific deployment guidance for this fork. The docs should make it obvious that Amica is the VRM conduit and PSFN Framework/Satellite Hub own companion intelligence and device orchestration.

Include:
- Required Amica env vars for conduit mode, bridge URL/token, PSFN/hub channel/session ids, model/animation defaults, face tracking, render tuning.
- Matching Satellite Hub env vars such as AMICA_BRIDGE_URL, AMICA_BRIDGE_TOKEN, AMICA_BRIDGE_OWNER_MODE, PSFN channel settings, mic control URL if applicable.
- Matching Framework requirements for psfn-amica external channel profiles, author metadata, API key, and channel privacy.
- Which legacy Amica features are intentionally disabled in conduit mode and where fallback/debug mode still exists.
- Manual smoke steps and troubleshooting for no audio, wrong channel id, SSE auth, mic mute proxy, and blank VRM canvas.
acceptance_criteria: A PSFN operator can configure Framework, Satellite Hub, and Amica from the docs without reading source code. The docs explicitly list ownership boundaries and known unresolved limitations. README or docs link to the new PSFN-specific guidance.
close_reason: Expanded PSFN conduit deployment docs and linked them from README

title: Add end-to-end PSFN conduit smoke script
description:
Create a practical smoke workflow for a developer to prove Amica can act as the PSFN VRM conduit against a fake or real Satellite Hub. This should be separate from pure unit tests and should run locally with clear logging.

Minimum fake-hub path:
- Start Amica dev server in conduit env.
- Connect to /api/satelliteBridge SSE or websocket path, depending on the selected contract.
- Post a user.final event, an assistant.segment/final event with small fixture audio, an interrupt, and face.target/face.clear.
- Verify browser-visible text state, speaking state transitions where observable, and no local provider calls.

Optional visual path:
- Use Playwright only if added to the repo or already available.
- Screenshot/canvas-pixel checks can validate the VRM canvas is nonblank, but should not block early contract work if test infra is not ready.
acceptance_criteria: A documented npm script or test command can run a local fake bridge/hub smoke and print pass/fail diagnostics for user/assistant event flow, audio event acceptance, interrupt, and face target delivery. The smoke does not require live PSFN provider keys.
close_reason: Added fake Hub realtime smoke and VRM controls smoke under npm run smoke:psfn-conduit

title: Reduce production bundle and dependency surface
description:
After conduit-mode behavior is fenced, reduce the amount of unused Amica AI stack loaded in production PSFN deployments. The goal is a lean VRM conduit, not an immediate risky deletion of all upstream code.

Likely candidates to lazy-load, fence, or eventually remove from production bundles:
- Browser Whisper worker, Transformers.js, VAD, onnxruntime-web paths for local STT.
- SpeechT5 worker/assets and local/remote TTS provider code.
- Moshi, Window.ai, Ollama, llama.cpp, kobold, OpenRouter, Arbius provider surfaces.
- External social API clients and plugin/news/function-calling paths.
- Unused docs/settings/provider pages from production route if they still inflate bundle.

Do this only after config/UI/chat ownership is clear so removal does not break retained vision/face tracking/VRM features.
acceptance_criteria: PSFN conduit builds do not eagerly load local STT/TTS/LLM/provider workers or heavyweight provider modules on the default page. A bundle/build check or documented analysis shows the retained large assets are for VRM/rendering/camera/face tracking, not unused AI backends. Legacy mode remains buildable unless a later deletion bead explicitly removes it.
close_reason: Lazy-loaded legacy AI providers/helpers from chat module and added lazy import smoke check

title: Constrain legacy HTTP/SSE bridge compatibility path
description:
Make src/pages/api/satelliteBridge and src/features/psfnSatelliteBridge a stable Amica-side contract for Satellite Hub. The current bridge works as a minimal POST-to-SSE relay but needs production hardening and contract clarity.

Current code:
- POST /api/satelliteBridge requires AMICA_BRIDGE_TOKEN and validates user.final, assistant.segment/final, interrupt, face.target, face.clear.
- GET /api/satelliteBridge opens SSE without token validation.
- Audio payloads are base64 with a hardcoded 2 MB limit.
- Events use data.sessionId, turnId, optional segmentIndex.
- face.target/clear are consumed by FaceTrackingBridge, while Chat ignores face events.

Work scope:
- Apply the resolved channel/session naming from the contract decision.
- Decide whether SSE GET needs token/auth and implement it if required.
- Validate event ordering, segmentIndex, mimeType, max payload sizes, and unknown fields consistently.
- Add keepalive/reconnect behavior as needed for browser and Tauri.
- Keep the bridge generic enough for expression/animation/viseme/vision events if the contract selects HTTP+SSE.
acceptance_criteria: Bridge API rejects malformed or unauthorized POSTs, emits only validated events, has clear auth behavior for GET/SSE, uses the agreed id fields, and includes Jest/API-level tests for accepted/rejected user, assistant, interrupt, face, and future extension events. Satellite Hub can target the endpoint without relying on undocumented behavior.
close_reason: Legacy bridge validator/API tests added and compatibility path constrained

comments.author: axAilotl
comments.text: Reframed after building-satellites.md review: this bead now covers keeping the existing HTTP/SSE bridge constrained and safe as a compatibility/development path. It is no longer on the critical path for PSFN Hub integration.

title: Create migration cleanup plan for legacy Amica AI surfaces
description:
After the PSFN conduit mode is working and tested, decide what legacy Amica code can be removed rather than merely hidden. This is intentionally later because deleting provider code too early can make it harder to compare behavior or recover useful presentation features.

Audit candidates:
- Provider settings pages and provider implementations that are never used in PSFN mode.
- docs/guides for local AI providers that do not apply to this fork.
- workers/assets for local STT/TTS that are not needed for face tracking or vision.
- external API/social/plugin systems if PSFN Framework fully replaces them.
- Tauri packaging or PWA behavior if production deployment selects only one.

Keep candidates:
- VRM, animation, lip sync, emote/expression, model store, camera, face tracking, render/debug tooling, and practical import/model management.
acceptance_criteria: A follow-on cleanup checklist identifies exact files/modules/dependencies to delete, keep, or leave legacy-only. Each proposed deletion has a reason and a verification command. No deletion is performed until the conduit mode has passing tests and smoke coverage.
